user_message: turn this GitHub repo into a skill for our agent system
good_response: Route to devops_agent. Research the repo's architecture, evaluate integration potential, then scaffold a skill.json manifest + SKILL.md methodology doc in backend/skills/<name>/. Reference only tools that exist in TOOL_REGISTRY.
bad_response: Route to knowledge_agent. Search the docs for information about how skills work, then tell the user what a skill is.
why_good_is_better: devops_agent creates files and scaffolds projects
knowledge_agent only reads/searches — can't create new packages
Good response specifies actionable build steps, not just information retrieval
Good response references real tool validation (TOOL_REGISTRY)

user_message: knowledge_agent keeps crashing when messages are routed to it
good_response: Route to self_healer_agent for initial diagnosis. Check logs for the ValueError from create_agent(), then identify that knowledge_agent is missing from ALL_AGENT_DEFINITIONS. Escalate to code_review_agent for the fix — adding the agent definition to backend/agents/__init__.py.
bad_response: Route to knowledge_agent. Try to process the message directly, which will crash again with the same ValueError.
good_plan: Escalate to code_review for definition fix
why_good_is_better: Diagnoses root cause instead of retrying the crash
Uses multi-agent handoff: self_healer diagnoses, code_review fixes
Bad response routes to THE BROKEN AGENT, causing the same crash
Good response follows fault remediation → fix pipeline

user_message: how powerful is our system right now and what can it do
good_response: Route to knowledge_agent. Perform comprehensive scan of ALL_AGENT_DEFINITIONS (agent count, roles, tiers), TOOL_REGISTRY (tool count, types), route files (endpoint count), and pipeline modules (content, webgen, browser). Produce structured capability report with counts and descriptions.
bad_response: Route to soul_core. Reflect on the system's purpose and values, produce philosophical response about what the system aspires to be.
good_plan: Scan agent registry for count and roles
Scan tool registry for tool inventory
Count HTTP endpoints across all route files
why_good_is_better: User asked WHAT the system CAN DO — factual, not philosophical
knowledge_agent retrieves and enumerates from the codebase
soul_core would reflect on purpose/values, not inventory capabilities
Good response produces concrete counts and descriptions

user_message: find all unused code that should be wired in
good_response: Route to code_review_agent. Scan for: (1) references to entities not defined (ghost agents), (2) route files not mounted in server.py, (3) tools in registry without agent permissions, (4) outdated documentation (CLAUDE.md counts vs actual). Report each with severity and fix recommendation.
bad_response: Route to devops_agent. Run a linter to check for import errors, then report any syntax issues.
good_plan: Grep for agent references vs definitions
Compare route imports vs registered routers
Audit tool registry vs agent tool_permissions
why_good_is_better: code_review_agent understands architectural patterns and governance
devops_agent checks builds, not architectural dead code
Good response identifies 4 specific categories of unused code
Bad response only catches syntax issues, not semantic dead code

user_message: create a temporary memory for agents to hand off context to each other
good_response: Route to soul_core for architectural design. Design a handoff memory system with: write_handoff(from_agent, to_agent, payload, ttl) for sending, read_handoffs(agent_id, consume=True) for receiving with auto-expiry. Store in data/agents/handoffs/pending.json. Respects INV-4 while enabling controlled cross-agent communication.
bad_response: Route to data_agent. Create a new SQLite table for agent messages with INSERT and SELECT operations.
good_plan: Design handoff schema respecting governance invariants
Add write_handoff to MemoryStore with TTL
why_good_is_better: soul_core designs inter-agent communication primitives (architectural decision)
data_agent handles ETL/schema, not memory system design
Good response integrates with existing MemoryStore (consistency)
Bad response creates separate storage layer (fragmentation)
Good response includes TTL auto-expiry and governance compliance

user_message: set up cron jobs that search the web for tech news without paid subscriptions
good_response: Route to devops_agent. Configure 3 scheduler jobs: morning digest (07:00 UTC) scanning HN/TechCrunch/Verge/GitHub Trending RSS, evening scan (19:00 UTC) for breaking news, weekly roundup (Sunday 10:00 UTC). All use free RSS feeds. Route to knowledge_agent for processing.
bad_response: Route to monitor_agent. Set up health check polling for news websites every 5 minutes.
good_plan: Add 3 cron jobs to server.py lifespan
morning: daily digest from RSS feeds
why_good_is_better: devops_agent configures automation and scheduling
monitor_agent checks service health, not fetches content
Good response uses proper cron expressions and RSS feeds
Bad response abuses health_check for content fetching (wrong tool)
Good response avoids 5-minute polling (resource waste)

user_message: check if our browser agent is secure while browsing the web
good_response: Route to security_agent. Audit browser automation layer for: SSRF protection (blocked prefixes list), URL scheme validation (http/https only), secret redaction in logs, session isolation per agent, TTL-based eviction. Report gaps and recommendations.
bad_response: Route to it_agent. Run a network scan to check if the browser ports are open.
why_good_is_better: security_agent analyzes application-level vulnerabilities
it_agent handles network infrastructure, not application security
Good response checks 5 specific security controls
Bad response checks network access, missing app-layer SSRF/injection risks
Browser security is code-level, not network-level

user_message: make sure our agents can browse the web without leaking our IP
good_response: Route to it_agent. Configure Tor SOCKS5 proxy or commercial VPN for Playwright browser sessions. Update browser launch args to use --proxy-server. Add DNS-over-HTTPS to prevent DNS leaks. Configure per-agent proxy rotation.
bad_response: Route to security_agent. Scan the browser code for IP address leaks in source files.
good_plan: Set up proxy rotation per agent
bad_plan: Scan source code for IP strings
why_good_is_better: it_agent configures network infrastructure (proxies, VPN, DNS)
security_agent identifies vulnerabilities but doesn't configure network
Good response implements actual protection (proxy + DNS-over-HTTPS)
Bad response scans code for strings, doesn't implement protection
IP leak prevention is network configuration, not code scanning
